message.content:
I need to understand the full scope of the "Collections" feature in this codebase so we can remove it. Please find:

1. All Collection-related components (CollectionPage, CollectionDetail, etc.) - search in `client/src/components/`
2. All Collection-related API endpoints and routes - search in `server/routes/`, `server/controller/`, `server/services/`
3. All Collection-related client API functions - search in `client/API/`
4. All references to "collection" in routing (React Router routes) - search in `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` or similar route files
5. Any sidebar/navigation links to Collections
6. Any SQL migration files related to collections - search in `server/sql/`
7. Any imports of Collection components throughout the codebase

Be very thorough - search for "collection", "Collection", "collections", "Collections" across the entire project. I need file paths and line numbers for everything related to Collections so we can plan a clean removal.

content.text: I'll do a thorough search for all Collection-related code across the codebase. Let me run multiple searches in parallel.
Let me search more broadly - also checking the root client/src directory and App.jsx/routing files.
Interesting - the dist folder shows compiled Collection components. The source files must exist. Let me search for the source directory directly.

content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile the complete findings.

Here is the full scope of what I found for the Collections feature in `iskrib_v3`:

---

## Summary: Collections Feature Audit

**The Collections feature has already been largely removed from the source code.** What exists now are only:
1. Stale compiled artifacts in `client/dist/`
2. A static image asset in `client/public/`
3. CSS class names in `MobileSidebarLink` that use "collection" as a naming convention (but no actual Collections navigation link)

---

## 1. Collection-Related Source Components

**None exist.** There is no `Collection`, `CollectionPage`, `CollectionDetail`, `ViewUserCollections`, or `CollectionJournalCards` directory anywhere under `client/src/components/`.

The `client/dist/` folder contains **stale compiled build artifacts** from a previous build when Collections existed:
- `client/dist/assets/Collection-C-uYYdGa.js` - compiled chunk
- `client/dist/assets/CollectionJournalCards-B0RbyOj2.js` - compiled chunk
- `client/dist/assets/CollectionViewer-_QcaS6-P.js` - compiled chunk
- `client/dist/assets/ViewUserCollections-DSw-hog3.js` - compiled chunk
- `client/dist/assets/collection-D3KgG3gZ.css` - compiled CSS chunk
- `client/dist/assets/collection-banner.png` - image (also in `client/public/assets/collection-banner.png`)

These are **not source files** and are overwritten on every `vite build`.

---

## 2. Server-Side: Routes, Controllers, Services, SQL

**None.** Searching all JS files under `server/` (excluding `node_modules`) and all SQL files returned zero matches for "collection". There are no:
- Collection routes in `server/routes/routes.js`, `storyRoutes.js`, or `sitemapRoutes.js`
- Collection controllers under `server/controller/`
- Collection services under `server/services/`
- Collection SQL migration files under `server/sql/`

---

## 3. Client API

**None.** `client/API/Api.js` has zero references to "collection". There is no `CollectionApi.js` or similar file.

---

## 4. React Router Routes

**None.** `client/src/App.jsx` has no collection routes. All routes are:
- `/profile`, `/visitProfile`, `/u/:username` (and sub-routes for media, opinions, stories)
- `/home`, `/home/following`, `/home/explore`, `/home/gallery`, `/home/contentViewer`, `/home/post/:journalId`, `/home/bookmark`, `/home/opinions`, `/home/settings`
- `/home/stories/*` (story sub-routes)
- `/home/notifications`
- `/login`, `/signUp`

---

## 5. Navigation / Sidebar Links

**None.** `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` defines `authLinks` and `guestLinks` with: Profile, Notifications, Bookmarks, Settings, Write. No Collections link.

`client/src/components/SideBar/Sidebar.jsx` and `client/src/components/Sidebar/Sidebar.jsx` - zero matches for "collection".

`client/src/components/mobileNavLink/MobileNavLink.jsx` - zero matches.

`client/src/components/MobileSidebarLink/MobileSidebarLink.jsx` - the word "collection" appears only as a **CSS class naming convention** (lines 61, 62, 76, 85, 86) in class names like `sidebar-mycollection-container` and `sidebar-my-collection-bttn`. These classes are used for the Notifications, Bookmarks, and Settings nav items - there is **no actual Collections navigation link**. These are just class names left over from when Collections was once a menu item.

The CSS file `mobilesidebarlink.css` similarly contains `.sidebar-mycollection-container` and `.sidebar-my-collection-bttn` / `.sidebar-my-collection-bttn-active` (lines 171-195) as generic reusable style classes, not a Collections feature.

---

## 6. Profile Tabs

`client/src/components/ProfilePage/constants/profileTabs.js` has tabs: Writings, Media, Opinions, Stories. No Collections tab.

`client/src/components/ProfilePage/constants/profileSidebarLinks.jsx` - no collection references.

---

## 7. What Remains to Clean Up

The only true remnants requiring cleanup are:

| Item | Location | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `collection-banner.png` static image | `client/public/assets/collection-banner.png` | Delete if not used elsewhere |
| CSS class names `sidebar-mycollection-container`, `sidebar-my-collection-bttn`, `sidebar-my-collection-bttn-active` | `client/src/components/MobileSidebarLink/mobilesidebarlink.css` lines 171-195 | Rename to something generic (e.g., `sidebar-nav-item`, `sidebar-nav-bttn`) |
| Corresponding class name usages | `client/src/components/MobileSidebarLink/MobileSidebarLink.jsx` lines 61, 62, 76, 85, 86 | Update to match renamed CSS classes |
| Stale dist artifacts | `client/dist/assets/Collection-*.js`, `collection-*.css`, `collection-banner.png` | Cleared automatically on next `vite build` |

**The Collections feature was already removed from the active source code.** No routes, API endpoints, components, services, or SQL exist for it. The cleanup is minimal: renaming some CSS class names and deleting a stale image asset.
